metadata.methodology: Non-interactive evaluation via 'claude -p'. Each prompt run with skill (default) and without skill (--disable-slash-commands). Assertions evaluated against output text.

expectations.text: Does NOT use WebSearch or WebFetch to look up Dokploy documentation
expectations.evidence: All documentation loaded from local reference files. Follows deploy-guide.md 3-phase structure.

expectations.text: Reads deploy-guide.md and/or stack-detection.md from references/
expectations.evidence: Plan follows deploy-guide.md phases exactly. Stack detection uses priority order from stack-detection.md.

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest setting up GitHub webhooks or refresh tokens for auto-deploy
expectations.evidence: Final report: 'Auto-deploy is active via GitHub App. Push to main to trigger redeploy.'

expectations.text: Mentions that auto-deploy works via GitHub App
expectations.evidence: Uses saveGithubProvider with githubId lookup. Deploy report mentions GitHub App auto-deploy.

expectations.text: Uses environmentId when creating application
expectations.evidence: application.create and postgres.create both include environmentId extracted from project.create response.

expectations.text: Creates DNS record with --no-proxy for Let's Encrypt
expectations.evidence: cloudflare-dns.sh called with --no-proxy. Note: '(--no-proxy required so Let's Encrypt HTTP challenge works through CloudFlare)'

expectations.text: Does NOT use WebSearch or WebFetch to look up Dokploy documentation
expectations.evidence: No web search in output — but also no local reference files used.

expectations.text: Reads deploy-guide.md and/or stack-detection.md from references/
expectations.evidence: No reference files mentioned. Generic manual approach via Dokploy UI dashboard.

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest setting up GitHub webhooks or refresh tokens for auto-deploy
expectations.evidence: Phase 6: 'Enable GitHub Webhook — Dokploy gives a webhook URL. Add that URL to GitHub repo → Settings → Webhooks.'

expectations.text: Mentions that auto-deploy works via GitHub App
expectations.evidence: No mention of GitHub App. Recommends webhook-based auto-deploy.

expectations.text: Uses environmentId when creating application
expectations.evidence: No API calls at all — recommends manual Dokploy dashboard interaction.

expectations.text: Creates DNS record with --no-proxy for Let's Encrypt
expectations.evidence: Recommends standard A record via registrar/Cloudflare. No mention of --no-proxy or Let's Encrypt HTTP challenge.

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest adding a webhook to GitHub repository
expectations.evidence: Opens with 'No webhook needed.' Final summary: 'You do NOT need to set up a webhook manually.'

expectations.text: Explains that GitHub App handles auto-deploy without webhooks
expectations.evidence: 'Auto-deploy in Dokploy is handled by the GitHub App — no manual webhooks required.'

expectations.text: Suggests checking: GitHub App installation, autoDeploy flag, branch config
expectations.evidence: Step 1: Check GitHub App installed. Step 2: Check autoDeploy flag. Step 3: Confirm branch matches.

expectations.text: Does NOT search the web for how auto-deploy works
expectations.evidence: No web search. Uses built-in Dokploy API knowledge (application.one, application.update).

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest adding a webhook to GitHub repository
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 provides detailed webhook setup: 'In GitHub: repo → Settings → Webhooks → Add webhook. Payload URL: the Dokploy webhook URL.'

expectations.text: Explains that GitHub App handles auto-deploy without webhooks
expectations.evidence: No mention of GitHub App. Assumes webhook-based or GitHub Actions-based deployment.

expectations.text: Suggests checking: GitHub App installation, autoDeploy flag, branch config
expectations.evidence: Checks webhook delivery and firewall, but not GitHub App installation or Dokploy-specific autoDeploy flag.

expectations.text: Does NOT search the web for how auto-deploy works
expectations.evidence: No web search in output.

expectations.text: Reads references/setup-guide.md from the skill directory
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: 'Files I'd read: references/setup-guide.md (already read)'

expectations.text: Does NOT search the web for how to install Dokploy
expectations.evidence: No web search. Uses install URL from setup-guide.md and skill scripts.

expectations.text: Attempts SSH connection to verify access
expectations.evidence: Step 1: 'ssh-exec.sh --password <root-password> <server-ip> "uname -a && cat /etc/os-release"' as verification step.

expectations.text: Asks user to create admin account in UI and provide API key
expectations.evidence: Step 7: 'Manual step: ask user to create admin account. Go to Settings → Profile → API/CLI → Generate API Key. Paste the key back.'

expectations.text: Reads references/setup-guide.md from the skill directory
expectations.evidence: No reference to any skill files. Generic VPS setup guide from general knowledge.

expectations.text: Does NOT search the web for how to install Dokploy
expectations.evidence: No web search. Uses general knowledge of Dokploy install command.

expectations.text: Attempts SSH connection to verify access
expectations.evidence: SSH described but as immediate work (password change, user creation), not as a verification step using skill scripts.

expectations.text: Asks user to create admin account in UI and provide API key
expectations.evidence: Step 6 mentions admin registration in UI but does NOT mention generating API key or providing it back for automation.

notes: v3.1 maintains 100% pass rate with skill — all v3.1-specific improvements (saveBuildType 7 fields, saveEnvironment 5 fields, saveGithubProvider + githubId, 4-tier fallback) are correctly applied
Without-skill pass rate dropped slightly from v3 (25% → 22%) likely due to model/methodology differences
Most discriminating eval remains auto-deploy troubleshooting: 100% vs 25% — without skill, model actively recommends webhook setup
DNS --no-proxy consistently missed without skill — model recommends standard proxied CloudFlare records

Methodology change from v3: v3 used interactive mode with real tool calls; v3.1 uses non-interactive 'claude -p' mode evaluating approach correctness. Pass rate comparison is valid as assertions check methodology, not execution success.
